extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won from reduced sprint after FABBRO Matteo's leadout pace dropped most sprinters and GC riders in technical finale to Matera.
tactical_patterns: Groupama-FDJ used FABBRO to shred peloton in technical finale, creating selection that dropped traditional leadout trains before DÉMARE sprint.
Bora-Hansgrohe controlled despite SAGAN holding Ciclamino jersey, revealing points classification defense trumped stage win hunting even with headwinds favoring bunch.
Breakaway from three second-division Italian teams given 9.5min suggests WorldTour teams calculated headwind finale made escape mathematically impossible regardless of cooperation.

rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Won from technical reduced sprint after working hard himself final 2km, showing rare ability to both position and finish without full leadout
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Only 8th place despite Bora controlling race and holding Ciclamino, suggesting declining sprint speed even in favorable tactical scenarios
rider_signals.WHELAN James: positive: Second-year pro strongest in break on KOM climb, indicating climbing ability beyond typical breakaway filler role
rider_signals.FABBRO Matteo: positive: Paced so hard in technical finale that most sprinters and GC riders dropped, revealing unusual domestique firepower for reduced bunch sprints
